page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-4 is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with an x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The instance is equipped with 15.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.75 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU resources, and comes with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal strong single-core capabilities, with the instance scoring in the top tier for single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, alongside a strong Geekbench single-core score of 1916.0. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show limitations. Multi-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench falls into the lower tier, as do large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidths.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation and advanced instruction support, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions, provide solid value for workloads that can leverage hardware acceleration without requiring massive multi-core scaling. The instance is well-suited for single-threaded tasks, lightweight web serving, text processing, and moderate Redis deployments, but is less optimal for heavy database operations or parallelized software compilation.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-4 is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical Intel Xeon 8581C cores running at 2.3 GHz, paired with 15.0 GB of RAM. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core execution, particularly in single-threaded compression and text processing, though multi-core scaling and uncached memory bandwidth are relatively weak. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX for specialized vector and matrix math. Qualitatively, it offers a balanced resource profile for workloads that prioritize single-thread performance over massive parallelization, making it suitable for lightweight web serving, Redis caching, and basic text processing tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-4 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 4 vCPUs, 15 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, delivering strong single-core performance for lightweight web serving and text processing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-4 is an x86_64 server with 4 vCPUs and 15 GB RAM, offering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon cores with strong single-threaded performance and advanced matrix extension support.

Strong single-core performance in text processing and compression benchmarks
Supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Intel AMX
